<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.serena/memories/iterm2-font-configuration-fresh-install.md`

```text
     1→# iTerm2 Font Configuration on Fresh Install
     2→
     3→## Problem
     4→
     5→When running chezmoi on a fresh macOS install, the PlistBuddy command to set iTerm2's default profile font to MesloLGSNF-Regular 13 appeared to execute without errors, but the font wasn't actually applied in iTerm2. When opening iTerm2 for the first time, the Default profile would fall back to a system default font instead.
     6→
     7→### Root Cause
     8→
     9→The issue occurred because:
    10→
    11→1. Fonts are installed via Homebrew (`font-meslo-lg-nerd-font`) in `run_onchange_before_darwin-23-install-packages.sh.tmpl`
    12→2. Fonts are placed in `~/Library/Fonts/` by Homebrew
    13→3. **macOS font daemon (`fontd`) doesn't immediately register newly installed fonts**
    14→4. PlistBuddy command runs in `run_once_after_darwin-85-configure-system-defaults.sh.tmpl` and successfully sets the font preference
    15→5. **When iTerm2 launches for the first time, it can't find "MesloLGSNF-Regular" because fontd hasn't registered it yet**
    16→6. iTerm2 falls back to a default font
    17→
    18→### Timing Issue
    19→
    20→The problem only manifests on fresh installs when:
    21→- Fonts are installed for the first time
    22→- Configuration happens immediately after
    23→- No logout/restart has occurred
    24→- fontd hasn't naturally refreshed its font cache
    25→
    26→## Solution
    27→
    28→Created `run_once_after_darwin-84-refresh-font-cache.sh.tmpl` to restart the font daemon after font installation, forcing it to re-scan and register newly installed fonts before the PlistBuddy configuration runs.
    29→
    30→### Script Execution Order
    31→
    32→```
    33→23 → Install packages (includes fonts via Homebrew)
    34→   ↓
    35→84 → Refresh font cache (killall fontd - NEW SCRIPT)
    36→   ↓
    37→85 → Configure system defaults (PlistBuddy sets iTerm2 font)
    38→```
    39→
    40→### Implementation
    41→
    42→```bash
    43→{{- if eq .chezmoi.os "darwin" -}}
    44→#!/bin/bash
    45→
    46→source "{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/script-utils.sh"
    47→
    48→print_message "info" "Refreshing font cache to register newly installed fonts..."
    49→
    50→# Restart the font daemon (fontd) to re-scan installed fonts
    51→# Killing fontd is safe - macOS will automatically respawn it
    52→if killall fontd 2>/dev/null; then
    53→    print_message "success" "Font daemon restarted - new fonts are now available"
    54→    # Give fontd a moment to respawn and re-scan fonts
    55→    sleep 2
    56→else
    57→    print_message "warning" "Font daemon not found or could not restart"
    58→    print_message "info" "Fonts will be available after logout/login"
    59→fi
    60→
    61→{{ end -}}
    62→```
    63→
    64→### Technical Details
    65→
    66→- **Uses `killall fontd`**: Simple, reliable, and safe
    67→  - macOS automatically respawns fontd when killed
    68→  - fontd re-scans font directories on startup
    69→- **Why not `launchctl kickstart`**: Modern macOS blocks this for system services due to SIP (System Integrity Protection)
    70→- **Why not `atsutil`**: Deprecated by Apple and warned against in documentation
    71→- **2-second delay**: Gives fontd time to fully respawn and scan fonts
    72→- **Error handling**: Shows fallback message if fontd restart fails
    73→
    74→### PlistBuddy Font Configuration
    75→
    76→The font is set in `run_once_after_darwin-85-configure-system-defaults.sh.tmpl`:
    77→
    78→```bash
    79→# iTerm2: Set default profile font to MesloLGSNF size 13
    80→# Note: This requires iTerm2 to be quit first, otherwise changes may be overwritten
    81→if pgrep -q "iTerm2"; then
    82→    print_message "warning" "iTerm2 is running - font changes may not persist until restart"
    83→fi
    84→
    85→if /usr/libexec/PlistBuddy -c "Set 'New Bookmarks':0:'Normal Font' 'MesloLGSNF-Regular 13'" ~/Library/Preferences/com.googlecode.iterm2.plist 2>/dev/null; then
    86→    print_message "success" "iTerm2 font set to MesloLGSNF-Regular 13"
    87→else
    88→    print_message "warning" "Could not set iTerm2 font (profile may not exist yet)"
    89→fi
    90→```
    91→
    92→### Alternative Solution: Dynamic Profiles
    93→
    94→An alternative approach using iTerm2's Dynamic Profiles feature is also available at:
    95→`home/private_Library/private_Application Support/iTerm2/DynamicProfiles/DefaultProfile.json.tmpl`
    96→
    97→Dynamic Profiles have advantages:
    98→- Font resolution happens when iTerm2 launches, not during chezmoi apply
    99→- No timing issues with font cache
   100→- Officially supported by iTerm2
   101→- Changes apply immediately without restarting iTerm2
   102→
   103→However, Dynamic Profiles create a new profile instead of modifying the built-in Default profile.
   104→
   105→## Key Learnings
   106→
   107→### macOS Font Cache Management
   108→
   109→1. **Font Installation Locations**:
   110→   - `~/Library/Fonts/` - User-specific (default for Homebrew casks)
   111→   - `/Library/Fonts/` - System-wide (requires `--fontdir=/Library/Fonts` flag)
   112→
   113→2. **Font Registration**:
   114→   - `fontd` daemon manages font registration on macOS
   115→   - New fonts require fontd to be restarted or system logout/restart
   116→   - Font cache is automatically rebuilt when fontd starts
   117→
   118→3. **Safe Font Cache Refresh Methods**:
   119→   - `killall fontd` - Safest and most reliable (automatic respawn)
   120→   - `sudo atsutil databases -remove` + logout/restart (deprecated)
   121→   - Safe boot (hold Shift during startup) - rebuilds all caches
   122→
   123→4. **System Integrity Protection (SIP)**:
   124→   - Modern macOS protects system services from `launchctl kickstart`
   125→   - Font daemon (`fontd`) is SIP-protected
   126→   - `killall` works because it doesn't require privileged operations
   127→
   128→## Related Files
   129→
   130→- Font installation: `home/.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_before_darwin-23-install-packages.sh.tmpl`
   131→- Font cache refresh: `home/.chezmoiscripts/run_once_after_darwin-84-refresh-font-cache.sh.tmpl`
   132→- iTerm2 configuration: `home/.chezmoiscripts/run_once_after_darwin-85-configure-system-defaults.sh.tmpl`
   133→- Font package: `home/.chezmoidata/packages.yaml` (contains `font-meslo-lg-nerd-font`)
   134→- Dynamic Profile alternative: `home/private_Library/private_Application Support/iTerm2/DynamicProfiles/DefaultProfile.json.tmpl`
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→# Task Completion Workflow
     2→
     3→## Post-Development Actions
     4→
     5→When completing any development task in this repository, follow these steps:
     6→
     7→### 1. Template Testing (if applicable)
     8→```bash
     9→# Test template execution for modified templates
    10→chezmoi execute-template < filename.tmpl
    11→
    12→# Test template scripts
    13→cat {script-name}.tmpl | chezmoi execute-template
    14→```
    15→
    16→### 2. Apply Changes
    17→```bash
    18→# Apply specific changes
    19→chezmoi apply [specific-file]
    20→
    21→# Or apply all changes
    22→chezmoi apply
    23→```
    24→
    25→### 3. Validation
    26→```bash
    27→# Check what would change (dry run)
    28→chezmoi diff
    29→
    30→# Verify no syntax errors in shell scripts
    31→bash -n ~/path/to/script.sh
    32→```
    33→
    34→### 4. Testing Specific Components
    35→
    36→#### Shell Scripts (if modified)
    37→```bash
    38→# Source and test shared utilities
    39→source "$(chezmoi source-path)/scripts/shared-utils.sh"
    40→
    41→# Test specific functions
    42→print_message "info" "Test message"
    43→command_exists "chezmoi"
    44→```
    45→
    46→### 5. System Integration Testing
    47→
    48→#### Package Management
    49→```bash
    50→# Verify Homebrew packages
    51→brew bundle check --file ~/.brewfiles/current
    52→
    53→# Update if needed
    54→brew bundle --file ~/.brewfiles/current
    55→```
    56→
    57→#### Environment Validation
    58→```bash
    59→# Check development environments
    60→rustup --version      # Rust
    61→nvm list             # Node.js
    62→sdk list java        # Java (SDKMAN)
    63→sdk current          # Current SDK versions
    64→uv --version         # Python
    65→```
    66→
    67→### 6. Git Workflow
    68→```bash
    69→# Stage changes
    70→git add .
    71→
    72→# Commit with descriptive message
    73→git commit -m "descriptive message
    74→
    75→🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
    76→
    77→Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>"
    78→
    79→# Push changes (if ready)
    80→git push
    81→```
    82→
    83→## Important Notes
    84→
    85→### No Linting/Formatting Commands
    86→This repository does not have traditional linting or formatting commands since it's primarily configuration files and shell scripts managed by chezmoi templates.
    87→
    88→### Validation Methods
    89→- **Templates**: Use `chezmoi execute-template`
    90→- **Shell Scripts**: Use `bash -n` for syntax checking
    91→- **Configuration**: Use `chezmoi diff` to preview changes
    92→
    93→### Prerequisites for Success
    94→- KeePassXC database must be accessible for credential-dependent templates
    95→- Required tools must be installed for script execution (checked via shared utilities)
    96→- Machine tags must be properly configured for tag-dependent installations
    97→
    98→### Recovery Actions
    99→If something goes wrong:
   100→```bash
   101→# Revert to last known good state
   102→chezmoi git reset --hard HEAD~1
   103→
   104→# Or pull from remote
   105→chezmoi update
   106→
   107→# Check system state
   108→chezmoi doctor
   109→```
```

 `./CLAUDE.md`

```text
     1→
     2→# Chezmoi Dotfiles
     3→
     4→Quick reference guide for AI assistants working with this chezmoi dotfiles repository.
     5→
     6→## Quick Navigation
     7→
     8→### Essential Files
     9→- **OpenSpec specs**: `openspec/specs/` - Capability specifications and design
    10→- **Quick reference**: `.serena/memories/chezmoi-dotfiles-quick-reference.md` - Fast lookup
    11→
    12→### Key Directories
    13→- `home/.chezmoiscripts/` - Numbered setup scripts (execution order: 00-99)
    14→- `home/.chezmoidata/` - Static data files (packages.yaml, config.yaml)
    15→- `home/.chezmoitemplates/` - Reusable template components
    16→- `home/scripts/shared-utils.sh` - Common script functions
    17→
    18→## Common Commands
    19→
    20→```bash
    21→chezmoi apply                  # Apply changes to home directory
    22→chezmoi diff                   # Preview changes before applying
    23→chezmoi update                 # Pull latest from repo and apply
    24→chezmoi status                 # Show files that would change
    25→chezmoi add ~/.<file>          # Add a dotfile to source state
    26→chezmoi edit ~/.<file>         # Edit the source version of a file
    27→chezmoi cd                     # Open shell in source directory
    28→chezmoi managed                # List all managed files
    29→chezmoi cat ~/path/to/file     # Preview generated file content (verify templates/modify_ scripts)
    30→```
    31→
    32→## Chezmoi File Attributes
    33→
    34→Prefixes in source filenames control how chezmoi processes them:
    35→- **`private_`** - Sensitive/machine-specific files (restricted permissions)
    36→- **`dot_`** - Creates dotfiles (hidden files starting with `.`)
    37→- **`executable_`** - Creates executable files (chmod +x)
    38→- **`symlink_`** - Creates symbolic links
    39→- **`.tmpl` suffix** - Template files processed by Go template engine
    40→
    41→## Source Directory Structure
    42→
    43→```
    44→.local/share/chezmoi/
    45→├── home/                           # Root for all managed files (.chezmoiroot)
    46→│   ├── .chezmoidata/              # Static data files (YAML/JSON/TOML)
    47→│   ├── .chezmoitemplates/         # Reusable template snippets
    48→│   ├── .chezmoiscripts/           # Automated setup scripts
    49→│   ├── .chezmoiexternal.toml.tmpl # External dependency definitions
    50→│   ├── .chezmoi.toml.tmpl         # User configuration prompts
    51→│   └── [dotfiles with chezmoi prefixes]
    52→├── scripts/                        # Shared utility scripts
    53→├── hooks/                          # Pre/post operation hooks
    54→├── brewfiles/                      # Machine-specific Homebrew bundles
    55→└── remote_install.sh              # One-command bootstrap script
    56→```
    57→
    58→## Practical Quick Reference
    59→
    60→### Script Naming Pattern
    61→```
    62→{frequency}_{timing}_{os}-{order}-{description}.sh.tmpl
    63→```
    64→
    65→**Examples:**
    66→- `run_once_before_darwin-20-install-sdkman.sh.tmpl` - One-time installation
    67→- `run_onchange_after_darwin-45-setup-github-auth.sh.tmpl` - Re-runs on changes
    68→
    69→**Execution order categories:** 00-09 System Foundation, 10-19 Toolchains, 20-29 Package Mgmt, 30-39 Env Managers, 40-49 Env Setup, 80-99 System Config
    70→
    71→### Shared Utilities
    72→
    73→**Source shared utilities in all scripts:**
    74→```bash
    75→source "{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/shared-utils.sh"
    76→```
    77→
    78→**Common functions:**
    79→- `print_message "info|success|warning|error|skip|tip" "message"` - Consistent output
    80→- `command_exists "command"` - Check availability
    81→- `require_tools "tool1" "tool2"` - Validate dependencies
    82→
    83→See `.serena/memories/code-style-quick-reference.md` for complete function list and usage examples.
    84→
    85→### Template Testing
    86→```bash
    87→# Test template execution
    88→chezmoi execute-template < filename.tmpl
    89→
    90→# Debug template scripts
    91→cat script-name.tmpl | chezmoi execute-template
    92→```
    93→
    94→### Template Best Practices
    95→
    96→**Partial file management (modify_ scripts):**
    97→Use `modify_` prefix for files where you only manage specific keys (e.g., JSON configs modified by applications at runtime).
    98→- `modify_<filename>.tmpl` — bash script receiving current file on stdin, outputs merged result
    99→- Use jq for JSON merging: `echo "$existing" | jq --argjson servers "$managed" '.mcpServers = $servers'`
   100→- `modify_` scripts CAN use `.tmpl` extension (for chezmoi template expansion)
   101→- `chezmoi:modify-template` directive files must NOT use `.tmpl` extension (different mechanism)
   102→- Example: `modify_claude_desktop_config.json.tmpl` manages only `mcpServers`, preserves app-managed `preferences`
   103→
   104→**Command validation:**
   105→```go-template
   106→{{- if lookPath "rustup" }}
   107→  source "$HOME/.cargo/env"
   108→{{- end }}
   109→```
   110→⚠️ **Never** use `output "command" "-v"` - it fails if command doesn't exist. Use `lookPath` instead.
   111→
   112→**Architecture detection:**
   113→```go-template
   114→{{- if and (eq .chezmoi.os "darwin") (eq .chezmoi.arch "arm64") -}}
   115→```
   116→
   117→**Platform-specific scripts:**
   118→```go-template
   119→{{- if eq .chezmoi.os "darwin" -}}
   120→#!/bin/bash
   121→# script content
   122→{{ end -}}
   123→```
   124→
   125→### Machine-Specific Settings
   126→
   127→**Access machine config:**
   128→```go-template
   129→{{- $brewfilePath := includeTemplate "machine-brewfile-path" . }}
   130→{{- $sshEntry := includeTemplate "machine-config" (merge (dict "setting" "keepassxc_entries.ssh") .) }}
   131→```
   132→
   133→See `openspec/specs/machine-config/` for complete machine configuration system documentation.
   134→
   135→## Key Principles
   136→
   137→1. **No hardcoded secrets** - All credentials via KeePassXC
   138→2. **Data files are static** - Files in `.chezmoidata/` cannot be templates
   139→3. **Four-layer package management** (Homebrew, UV, Bun, SDKMAN) - See `openspec/specs/package-management/`
   140→4. **Consistent messaging** - Always use shared utilities for script output
   141→5. **Platform wrapping** - Darwin scripts must use conditional templates
   142→6. **Script ordering** - Machine-specific Brewfile (position 28) must always be last in the package management group (20-29)
   143→
   144→### Platform Requirements
   145→- **Target OS**: macOS 11.0+ (Big Sur or later)
   146→- **Architectures**: ARM64 (Apple Silicon) primary, x64 (Intel) secondary
   147→- **Bootstrap dependencies**: Xcode CLI Tools, KeePassXC (secrets), Homebrew (packages)
   148→
   149→### Tag Combinations
   150→- **Minimal**: `core`
   151→- **Developer**: `core,dev,ai`
   152→- **Work machine**: `core,dev,work`
   153→- **Personal machine**: `core,dev,ai,personal,datascience`
   154→
   155→## Claude Code Automations
   156→
   157→### Hooks (`.claude/settings.json`)
   158→- **Template validation** (PostToolUse): Runs `chezmoi execute-template` on `.tmpl` files after edits
   159→- **Data file protection** (PreToolUse): Blocks `{{ }}` expressions in `.chezmoidata/` files
   160→
   161→### Skills
   162→- `/new-script` - Generate a chezmoi script with correct naming, boilerplate, and conventions
   163→- `/apply` - Safe diff-then-apply workflow with confirmation
   164→
   165→### Agents
   166→- `template-reviewer` - Scans all templates for convention violations (read-only)
   167→
   168→### MCP Server Configuration
   169→- **Global MCP servers**: `home/private_dot_config/claude-extend/tools.json.tmpl` (deployed to `~/.config/claude-extend/tools.json`)
   170→- **Project MCP servers**: `.mcp.json` (if needed)
   171→- GitHub MCP uses `gh auth token` for authentication — no PAT management needed
   172→
   173→## When to Reference openspec/
   174→
   175→- **Package management** → `openspec/specs/package-management/`
   176→- **Machine config** → `openspec/specs/machine-config/`
   177→- **Secret management** → `openspec/specs/secret-management/`
   178→- **Major changes** → See archived proposals in `openspec/changes/archive/`
   179→
```

    10→# Added (renamed) on 2025-04-07

    13→# whether the project is in read-only mode

    16→read_only: false

    18→# list of tool names to exclude. We recommend not excluding any tools, see the readme for more details.
    19→# Below is the complete list of tools for convenience.
    20→# To make sure you have the latest list of tools, and to view their descriptions, 
    21→# execute `uv run scripts/print_tool_overview.py`.
    22→#
    23→#  * `activate_project`: Activates a project by name.
    24→#  * `check_onboarding_performed`: Checks whether project onboarding was already performed.
    25→#  * `create_text_file`: Creates/overwrites a file in the project directory.
    26→#  * `delete_lines`: Deletes a range of lines within a file.
    27→#  * `delete_memory`: Deletes a memory from Serena's project-specific memory store.
    28→#  * `execute_shell_command`: Executes a shell command.
    29→#  * `find_referencing_code_snippets`: Finds code snippets in which the symbol at the given location is referenced.
    30→#  * `find_referencing_symbols`: Finds symbols that reference the symbol at the given location (optionally filtered by type).
    31→#  * `find_symbol`: Performs a global (or local) search for symbols with/containing a given name/substring (optionally filtered by type).
    32→#  * `get_current_config`: Prints the current configuration of the agent, including the active and available projects, tools, contexts, and modes.
    33→#  * `get_symbols_overview`: Gets an overview of the top-level symbols defined in a given file.
    34→#  * `initial_instructions`: Gets the initial instructions for the current project.
    35→#     Should only be used in settings where the system prompt cannot be set,
    36→#     e.g. in clients you have no control over, like Claude Desktop.
    37→#  * `insert_after_symbol`: Inserts content after the end of the definition of a given symbol.
    38→#  * `insert_at_line`: Inserts content at a given line in a file.
    39→#  * `insert_before_symbol`: Inserts content before the beginning of the definition of a given symbol.
    40→#  * `list_dir`: Lists files and directories in the given directory (optionally with recursion).
    41→#  * `list_memories`: Lists memories in Serena's project-specific memory store.
    42→#  * `onboarding`: Performs onboarding (identifying the project structure and essential tasks, e.g. for testing or building).
    43→#  * `prepare_for_new_conversation`: Provides instructions for preparing for a new conversation (in order to continue with the necessary context).
    44→#  * `read_file`: Reads a file within the project directory.
    45→#  * `read_memory`: Reads the memory with the given name from Serena's project-specific memory store.
    46→#  * `remove_project`: Removes a project from the Serena configuration.
    47→#  * `replace_lines`: Replaces a range of lines within a file with new content.
    48→#  * `replace_symbol_body`: Replaces the full definition of a symbol.
    49→#  * `restart_language_server`: Restarts the language server, may be necessary when edits not through Serena happen.
    50→#  * `search_for_pattern`: Performs a search for a pattern in the project.
    51→#  * `summarize_changes`: Provides instructions for summarizing the changes made to the codebase.
    52→#  * `switch_modes`: Activates modes by providing a list of their names
    53→#  * `think_about_collected_information`: Thinking tool for pondering the completeness of collected information.
    54→#  * `think_about_task_adherence`: Thinking tool for determining whether the agent is still on track with the current task.
    55→#  * `think_about_whether_you_are_done`: Thinking tool for determining whether the task is truly completed.
    56→#  * `write_memory`: Writes a named memory (for future reference) to Serena's project-specific memory store.
    57→excluded_tools: []
    58→
    59→# initial prompt for the project. It will always be given to the LLM upon activating the project
    60→# (contrary to the memories, which are loaded on demand).
    61→initial_prompt: ""
    62→# the name by which the project can be referenced within Serena
    63→project_name: "chezmoi"
    64→
    65→
    66→# list of languages for which language servers are started; choose from:
    67→#   al                  bash                clojure             cpp                 csharp
    68→#   csharp_omnisharp    dart                elixir              elm                 erlang
    69→#   fortran             fsharp              go                  groovy              haskell

    74→#   terraform           toml                typescript          typescript_vts      vue

    76→#   (This list may be outdated. For the current list, see values of Language enum here:

    78→#   For some languages, there are alternative language servers, e.g. csharp_omnisharp, ruby_solargraph.)
    79→# Note:
    80→#   - For C, use cpp
    81→#   - For JavaScript, use typescript
    82→#   - For Free Pascal/Lazarus, use pascal
    83→# Special requirements:

    86→# When using multiple languages, the first language server that supports a given file will be used for that file.

 `./CLAUDE.md`

```text
     1→
     2→# Chezmoi Dotfiles
     3→
     4→Quick reference guide for AI assistants working with this chezmoi dotfiles repository.
     5→
     6→## Quick Navigation
     7→
     8→### Essential Files
     9→- **OpenSpec specs**: `openspec/specs/` - Capability specifications and design
    10→- **Code style examples**: `.serena/memories/code-style-quick-reference.md` - Extended cookbook
    11→
    12→### Key Directories
    13→- `home/.chezmoiscripts/` - Numbered setup scripts (execution order: 00-99)
    14→- `home/.chezmoidata/` - Static data files (packages.yaml, config.yaml)
    15→- `home/.chezmoitemplates/` - Reusable template components
    16→- `home/scripts/shared-utils.sh` - Common script functions
    17→
    18→## Common Commands
    19→
    20→```bash
    21→chezmoi apply                  # Apply changes to home directory
    22→chezmoi diff                   # Preview changes before applying
    23→chezmoi update                 # Pull latest from repo and apply
    24→chezmoi status                 # Show files that would change
    25→chezmoi add ~/.<file>          # Add a dotfile to source state
    26→chezmoi edit ~/.<file>         # Edit the source version of a file
    27→chezmoi cd                     # Open shell in source directory
    28→chezmoi managed                # List all managed files
    29→chezmoi cat ~/path/to/file     # Preview generated file content (verify templates/modify_ scripts)
    30→```
    31→
    32→## Chezmoi File Attributes
    33→
    34→Prefixes in source filenames control how chezmoi processes them:
    35→- **`private_`** - Sensitive/machine-specific files (restricted permissions)
    36→- **`dot_`** - Creates dotfiles (hidden files starting with `.`)
    37→- **`executable_`** - Creates executable files (chmod +x)
    38→- **`symlink_`** - Creates symbolic links
    39→- **`.tmpl` suffix** - Template files processed by Go template engine
    40→
    41→## Source Directory Structure
    42→
    43→```
    44→.local/share/chezmoi/
    45→├── home/                           # Root for all managed files (.chezmoiroot)
    46→│   ├── .chezmoidata/              # Static data files (YAML/JSON/TOML)
    47→│   ├── .chezmoitemplates/         # Reusable template snippets
    48→│   ├── .chezmoiscripts/           # Automated setup scripts
    49→│   ├── .chezmoiexternal.toml.tmpl # External dependency definitions
    50→│   ├── .chezmoi.toml.tmpl         # User configuration prompts
    51→│   └── [dotfiles with chezmoi prefixes]
    52→├── scripts/                        # Shared utility scripts
    53→├── hooks/                          # Pre/post operation hooks
    54→├── brewfiles/                      # Machine-specific Homebrew bundles
    55→└── remote_install.sh              # One-command bootstrap script
    56→```
    57→
    58→## Practical Quick Reference
    59→
    60→### Script Naming Pattern
    61→```
    62→{frequency}_{timing}_{os}-{order}-{description}.sh.tmpl
    63→```
    64→
    65→**Examples:**
    66→- `run_once_before_darwin-20-install-sdkman.sh.tmpl` - One-time installation
    67→- `run_onchange_after_darwin-45-setup-github-auth.sh.tmpl` - Re-runs on changes
    68→
    69→**Script execution order:**
    70→- **05**: Rosetta 2 | **10**: Rust | **20**: SDKMAN | **23**: Homebrew packages | **24**: SDKMAN SDKs | **25**: UV tools | **26**: Bun packages | **28**: Machine-specific Brewfile
    71→- **30**: UV manager | **35**: nvm | **36**: Claude Code (`ai` tag)
    72→- **45**: GitHub auth | **80**: Microsoft Defender | **82**: Global Protect VPN | **83**: Atuin | **85**: System defaults | **90**: Hosts file | **95**: Syncthing | **97**: SSH test
    73→
    74→### Shared Utilities
    75→
    76→**Source shared utilities in all scripts:**
    77→```bash
    78→source "{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/shared-utils.sh"
    79→```
    80→
    81→**Common functions:**
    82→- `print_message "info|success|warning|error|skip|tip" "message"` - Consistent output
    83→- `command_exists "command"` - Check availability
    84→- `require_tools "tool1" "tool2"` - Validate dependencies
    85→- `wait_for_app_installation "/path/to/App.app" "App Name"` - Interactive install wait
    86→- `ensure_directory "path" ["sudo"]` - Create directory if missing
    87→- `prompt_ready ["message"]` - User prompt helper
    88→
    89→See `.serena/memories/code-style-quick-reference.md` for extended examples and patterns.
    90→
    91→### Template Testing
    92→```bash
    93→# Test template execution
    94→chezmoi execute-template < filename.tmpl
    95→
    96→# Debug template scripts
    97→cat script-name.tmpl | chezmoi execute-template
    98→```
    99→
   100→### Template Best Practices
   101→
   102→**Partial file management (modify_ scripts):**
   103→Use `modify_` prefix for files where you only manage specific keys (e.g., JSON configs modified by applications at runtime).
   104→- `modify_<filename>.tmpl` — bash script receiving current file on stdin, outputs merged result
   105→- Use jq for JSON merging: `echo "$existing" | jq --argjson servers "$managed" '.mcpServers = $servers'`
   106→- `modify_` scripts CAN use `.tmpl` extension (for chezmoi template expansion)
   107→- `chezmoi:modify-template` directive files must NOT use `.tmpl` extension (different mechanism)
   108→- Example: `modify_claude_desktop_config.json.tmpl` manages only `mcpServers`, preserves app-managed `preferences`
   109→
   110→**Command validation:**
   111→```go-template
   112→{{- if lookPath "rustup" }}
   113→  source "$HOME/.cargo/env"
   114→{{- end }}
   115→```
   116→⚠️ **Never** use `output "command" "-v"` - it fails if command doesn't exist. Use `lookPath` instead.
   117→
   118→**Architecture detection:**
   119→```go-template
   120→{{- if and (eq .chezmoi.os "darwin") (eq .chezmoi.arch "arm64") -}}
   121→```
   122→
   123→**Platform-specific scripts:**
   124→```go-template
   125→{{- if eq .chezmoi.os "darwin" -}}
   126→#!/bin/bash
   127→# script content
   128→{{ end -}}
   129→```
   130→
   131→### Reusable Templates
   132→
   133→Available in `home/.chezmoitemplates/`:
   134→- `machine-name` - Cross-platform machine name detection
   135→- `machine-config` - Machine-specific setting lookup (single property)
   136→- `machine-settings` - All machine settings as JSON dict (preferred for multiple lookups)
   137→- `icloud-account-id` - Returns iCloud account ID if signed in (macOS)
   138→
   139→**Access machine config:**
   140→```go-template
   141→{{- $brewfilePath := includeTemplate "machine-brewfile-path" . }}
   142→{{- $sshEntry := includeTemplate "machine-config" (merge (dict "setting" "keepassxc_entries.ssh") .) }}
   143→```
   144→
   145→See `openspec/specs/machine-config/` for complete machine configuration system documentation.
   146→
   147→## Key Principles
   148→
   149→1. **No hardcoded secrets** - All credentials via KeePassXC
   150→2. **Data files are static** - Files in `.chezmoidata/` cannot be templates
   151→3. **Four-layer package management** (Homebrew, UV, Bun, SDKMAN) - See `openspec/specs/package-management/`
   152→4. **Consistent messaging** - Always use shared utilities for script output
   153→5. **Platform wrapping** - Darwin scripts must use conditional templates
   154→6. **Script ordering** - Machine-specific Brewfile (position 28) must always be last in the package management group (20-29)
   155→
   156→### Platform Requirements
   157→- **Target OS**: macOS 11.0+ (Big Sur or later)
   158→- **Architectures**: ARM64 (Apple Silicon) primary, x64 (Intel) secondary
   159→- **Bootstrap dependencies**: Xcode CLI Tools, KeePassXC (secrets), Homebrew (packages)
   160→
   161→### Tag Combinations
   162→- **Minimal**: `core`
   163→- **Developer**: `core,dev,ai`
   164→- **Work machine**: `core,dev,work`
   165→- **Personal machine**: `core,dev,ai,personal,datascience`
   166→
   167→## Claude Code Automations
   168→
   169→### Hooks (`.claude/settings.json`)
   170→- **Template validation** (PostToolUse): Runs `chezmoi execute-template` on `.tmpl` files after edits
   171→- **Data file protection** (PreToolUse): Blocks `{{ }}` expressions in `.chezmoidata/` files
   172→
   173→### Skills
   174→- `/new-script` - Generate a chezmoi script with correct naming, boilerplate, and conventions
   175→- `/apply` - Safe diff-then-apply workflow with confirmation
   176→
   177→### Agents
   178→- `template-reviewer` - Scans all templates for convention violations (read-only)
   179→
   180→### MCP Server Configuration
   181→- **Global MCP servers**: `home/private_dot_config/claude-extend/tools.json.tmpl` (deployed to `~/.config/claude-extend/tools.json`)
   182→- **Project MCP servers**: `.mcp.json` (if needed)
   183→- GitHub MCP uses `gh auth token` for authentication — no PAT management needed
   184→
   185→## When to Reference openspec/
   186→
   187→- **Package management** → `openspec/specs/package-management/`
   188→- **Machine config** → `openspec/specs/machine-config/`
   189→- **Secret management** → `openspec/specs/secret-management/`
   190→- **Major changes** → See archived proposals in `openspec/changes/archive/`
   191→
```
